title: 10 best herbal capsules for peptic ulcers
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for peptic ulcers are dietary supplements that contain a variety of herbs known for their soothing and healing properties. These herbs, such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root, can help reduce inflammation, protect the lining of the stomach and duodenum, and promote the healing of ulcers. By taking these herbal capsules, individuals with peptic ulcers may experience relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, heartburn, and nausea. Furthermore, these natural remedies can provide an alternative to traditional medications, which may have side effects or contraindications. For example, a person with a history of stomach ulcers may find that taking herbal capsules helps them manage their symptoms and maintain a better quality of life, allowing them to engage in daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off H. pylori bacteria, which are common causes of peptic ulcers. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to stimulate the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, which can protect the lining of the stomach and duodenum, and promote the healing of existing ulcers. The anti-oxidant properties of aloe vera can also help reduce oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to the development of ulcers. Overall, aloe vera capsule can provide a natural and effective way to manage and treat peptic ulcers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from pain and discomfort caused by peptic ulcers. This is due to the presence of compounds like bradykinase, salicylic acid, and various polysaccharides.

Mucosal protective: Aloe vera forms a protective layer on the stomach lining, preventing acid and digestive enzymes from causing further damage to the ulcerated area. This is attributed to the polysaccharide content, particularly acemannan, in aloe vera.

Accelerated healing: Aloe vera promotes faster healing of peptic ulcers by stimulating the growth of new cells and reducing inflammation. This is facilitated by the presence of glycoproteins and vitamins A, C, and E in aloe vera.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the stomach, which can contribute to the development and worsening of peptic ulcers. The antioxidant properties are attributed to various compounds, including vitamins C and E, as well as flavonoids and tannins.

Antimicrobial: Aloe vera has antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with the development of peptic ulcers. The antimicrobial activity is due to the presence of anthraquinones, such as aloin and emodin, in aloe vera.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a gel-like substance that is rich in compounds with anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, making it effective for treating peptic ulcers.

Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a bitter sap that has been shown to stimulate the production of mucus in the digestive tract, which can help protect the lining of the stomach and duodenum from damage caused by acid and pepsin.

Seeds: The seeds of the aloe vera plant are a rich source of antioxidants and have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healing of peptic ulcers.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the aloe vera plant contain compounds with antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium that is commonly associated with the development of peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Purchase high-quality aloe vera leaves and herbal ingredients such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root, ensuring the correct dosages for treating peptic ulcers (e.g., 200mg aloe vera, 400mg slippery elm, 250mg marshmallow root, and 250mg licorice root per capsule).
Clean the aloe vera leaves, remove the spikes, and fillet them to extract the gel, then blend it and strain it to remove any remaining impurities.
Combine the strained aloe vera gel with the finely ground herbal ingredients, mixing them thoroughly in a bowl.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule-filling machine or by hand, ensuring each capsule contains the correct dosage.
Allow the filled capsules to dry in a cool, dark place for 24-48 hours before storing them in airtight containers and refrigerating them for up to two weeks or freezing for up to three months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has antibacterial properties. This compound helps to eradicate Helicobacter pylori, the bacteria responsible for causing most peptic ulcers. Additionally, barberry has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms such as pain, bloating, and indigestion. Barberry's ability to stimulate the production of bile and digestive enzymes also aids in digestion and can help prevent the formation of new ulcers. Overall, barberry's multifaceted therapeutic actions make it a valuable herbal remedy for managing peptic ulcers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Barberry capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by inflammation and irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property are berberine and oxycanthine.

Antimicrobial: Barberry capsules have antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of peptic ulcers. The main active constituents responsible for this property are berberine and oxycanthine.

Astringent: Barberry capsules can help reduce secretions and promote healing in the stomach and intestines, which is important for peptic ulcers as they can cause excessive stomach acid production. The astringent property comes from the tannins present in barberry.

Mucoprotective: Barberry capsules can protect the mucous membrane of the stomach and intestines, which is important for peptic ulcers as they can cause damage to these membranes. The active constituents responsible for this property are berberine and palmatine.

Antispasmodic: Barberry capsules can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for peptic ulcers as they can cause spasms and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property are berberine and oxycanthine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain berberine, an alkaloid that can help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, making it useful for treating peptic ulcers.
Rhizomes: Rich in berberine and other compounds that can help heal stomach lining and reduce pain associated with peptic ulcers.
Bark: Contains berberine and other alkaloids that can help kill harmful bacteria in the stomach, such as H. pylori, which can cause peptic ulcers.
Leaves: High in tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the stomach lining, making them useful for treating peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1,000 mg of dried common barberry bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with 500 mg of microcrystalline cellulose as a filler in a bowl.
Add a small amount of distilled water to the mixture and knead it into a dough-like consistency.
Form the dough into small, capsule-shaped pieces using a pill maker or by hand.
Allow the capsules to dry for 24 hours before storing them in a sealed container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains active compounds, such as carvol and carvone, which have anti-spasmodic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium that is commonly associated with peptic ulcers. Additionally, caraway has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help improve digestion and reduce the risk of developing ulcers. By promoting overall digestive health, caraway capsules can provide relief from the symptoms of peptic ulcers and support the healing process.
remedy_properties: Antiulcerogenic property: Caraway contains active constituents such as carvone and limonene, which have been shown to protect the stomach lining and promote the healing of ulcers. This is important for peptic ulcers as it helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the damaged mucosal tissue.

Antispasmodic property: Caraway has the ability to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps. This is important for peptic ulcers as it can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by spasms in the stomach and intestines.

Carminative property: Caraway helps to reduce bloating, gas, and other digestive issues. This is important for peptic ulcers as it can help alleviate symptoms associated with indigestion and promote overall digestive health.

Antioxidant property: Caraway contains antioxidants, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for peptic ulcers as it helps to prevent the formation of new ulcers and supports the healing process.

Bactericidal property: Caraway has been shown to have antibacterial properties, particularly against Helicobacter pylori, which is a major cause of peptic ulcers. This is important for peptic ulcers as it can help eliminate the underlying infection and promote long-term healing and prevention.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used for medicinal tea due to their volatile oil content, which includes carvone and limonene, known to aid in digestion and soothe the stomach lining, thus beneficial for peptic ulcers.

Leaves: Caraway leaves contain essential oils and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them suitable for treating peptic ulcers by protecting the mucosal lining of the stomach.

Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes have antispasmodic and carminative properties due to their richness in essential oils, which can help alleviate symptoms of peptic ulcers by reducing stomach spasms and gastric acid production.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried caraway seeds and 1 cup of water.
Grind caraway seeds into a fine powder, then mix with 1 cup of empty capsule casings.
Fill each capsule with the caraway seed powder, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dry place for up to six months.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common centaury capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains bitter principles that stimulate the production of digestive juices, including gastric acid, mucus, and bile. These substances help to protect the lining of the stomach and duodenum, reducing the risk of damage and ulceration. Additionally, centaury has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract. Studies have shown that centaury extract can inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium that is associated with the development of peptic ulcers. By addressing the underlying causes of peptic ulcers and promoting digestive health, centaury capsules can provide relief from symptoms such as pain, bloating, and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-secretory: Centaury contains alkaloids that help reduce gastric acid secretion, which is important for peptic ulcers as excessive acid production can irritate and damage the stomach lining.

Cytoprotective: Centaury extracts have been shown to protect the gastric mucosa, which is crucial for peptic ulcers as the mucosal lining needs to be preserved to allow the ulcer to heal. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins in centaury.
Anti-inflammatory: Centaury has anti-inflammatory properties due to its polyphenol content, which helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating pain and promoting ulcer healing.
Antioxidant: Centaury is rich in antioxidants that scavenge free radicals, which is significant for peptic ulcers as oxidative stress can exacerbate the ulceration process.

Antimicrobial: Centaury has antimicrobial properties, particularly against Helicobacter pylori, which is a primary cause of peptic ulcers. The presence of bitter principles in centaury, such as gentiopicroside, contributes to its antimicrobial activity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the common centaury capsule plant are used to make medicinal tea for peptic ulcers due to their bitter properties that can help stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining.

Flowers: The flowers of the common centaury capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for peptic ulcers due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining and promote healing.

Roots: The roots of the common centaury capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for peptic ulcers due to their astringent properties that can help tighten and heal damaged tissues in the stomach lining.
Stems: The stems of the common centaury capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for peptic ulcers due to their tonic properties that can help strengthen and support the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried common centaury herb and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with 0.5-1 teaspoon of a binding agent, such as gelatin or starch.
Using a capsule filling machine or by hand, fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place.
Store the completed centaury capsules in an airtight container, away from direct sunlight, and take 1-2 capsules daily as needed for peptic ulcer relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Curcumin works by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes that can damage the mucosal lining of the stomach and intestines, thereby preventing the formation of ulcers. Additionally, curcumin stimulates the production of mucin, a glycoprotein that forms a protective layer over the mucosal lining, providing an added layer of protection against acid and other digestive juices. Furthermore, curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with peptic ulcers. Overall, turmeric capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage and prevent peptic ulcers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by inflammation. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property.

Antioxidant: Turmeric capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage, which is important for peptic ulcers as they can cause damage to the stomach lining. The active constituent curcumin is also responsible for this property.

Antibacterial: Turmeric capsules have antibacterial properties that can help kill the H. pylori bacteria, which is often a cause of peptic ulcers. The active constituent curcumin, as well as other compounds in turmeric, are responsible for this property.

Gastroprotective: Turmeric capsules can help protect the stomach lining from damage, which is important for peptic ulcers as they can cause damage to the stomach lining. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property.

Wound healing: Turmeric capsules can help speed up the healing process of peptic ulcers. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property, as it can stimulate the growth of new tissue and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes contain curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce stomach acid and promote stomach lining healing in peptic ulcers.

Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacterium associated with peptic ulcers.

Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which may help protect the stomach lining and reduce the risk of peptic ulcers.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Gather and measure ingredients: 500mg turmeric powder, 100mg black pepper powder, and 100mg ginger powder for each capsule.
Mix turmeric, black pepper, and ginger powders thoroughly in a bowl.
Load the powder mixture into size 00 empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers, preferably in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules daily after meals or as directed by a healthcare professional for peptic ulcer treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains active compounds that can increase the mucus content in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby providing a protective lining to the stomach and duodenum. This helps prevent the acid from causing damage to the stomach and intestinal lining, promoting the healing of existing ulcers. Additionally, licorice has been shown to inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium that is known to cause peptic ulcers. By reducing the bacterial load and protecting the stomach lining, licorice capsules can help alleviate the symptoms of peptic ulcers and promote overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by inflammation and irritation. The active constituent that provides this property is glycyrrhizin.

Mucosal protective: Licorice capsules can help protect the stomach lining by stimulating the production of mucus and other protective agents, which is important for peptic ulcers as they involve damage to the stomach lining. The active constituents that provide this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antisecretory: Licorice capsules can reduce the production of stomach acid, which is important for peptic ulcers as excessive stomach acid can irritate and damage the stomach lining. The active constituent that provides this property is glycyrrhetic acid.

Antimicrobial: Licorice capsules have antimicrobial properties, which is important for peptic ulcers as they can be caused by the bacterium Helicobacter pylori. The active constituents that provide this property are flavonoids and glycyrrhizin.

Gastroprotective: Licorice capsules can help heal and prevent the recurrence of peptic ulcers by promoting the regeneration of stomach lining cells. The active constituent that provides this property is glycyrrhizin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice capsule plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help increase the mucus lining of the stomach, protecting it from acid and ulcers.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plant have similar properties to the roots and can help treat and prevent peptic ulcers.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice capsule plant contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in the healing of peptic ulcers.
Seeds: The seeds of the plant contain various compounds, including coumarins and triterpenoids, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects and can help in the treatment of peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 400 milligrams of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.
Mix the ground licorice root powder with 100% pure vegetarian capsules, filling approximately 12-15 capsules, allotting 20-30 minutes.
Thoroughly wash hands and any tools used in the process to maintain cleanliness and prevent contamination.
Store the completed licorice root capsules in a cool, dry place, ensuring a shelf life of up to six months.
Consult with a healthcare professional before consuming, as licorice may interact with certain medications and medical conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Immortelle capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory and healing agent called Chamazulene. This compound works by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining, promoting the healing of damaged tissues, and preventing the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause ulcers. Additionally, Immortelle has been shown to stimulate the production of prostaglandins, which protect the stomach lining and promote the secretion of digestive juices, thereby improving overall digestive health. Its antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals, reducing the risk of oxidative stress and damage to the stomach lining. All of these factors make Immortelle capsule an effective natural remedy for managing peptic ulcers and promoting digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Immutelle capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include chamazulene and matricin.

Antimicrobial: Immutelle capsules have antimicrobial properties that help kill the Helicobacter pylori bacteria, which is often present in people with peptic ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property include alpha-pinene and limonene.

Wound healing: Immutelle capsules have wound healing properties that help promote the healing of the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they cause sores in the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include azulenols and flavonoids.

Pain relief: Immutelle capsules have pain relief properties that help alleviate the pain associated with peptic ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property include chamazulene and matricin.

Protective: Immutelle capsules have protective properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by damage to the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and tannins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, immortelle flowers help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and promote healing of peptic ulcers.
Leaves: High in flavonoids and tannins, immortelle leaves can help alleviate pain, reduce acid secretion, and protect the stomach lining, aiding in the treatment of peptic ulcers.
Stems: Containing essential oils and phenolic acids, immortelle stems can help inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a major cause of peptic ulcers, and support overall digestive health.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, immortelle seeds can help soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and aid in the healing process of peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 teaspoons of dried immortelle flowers, 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, and 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root in a mixing bowl.
Grind the herbal mixture in a spice grinder or coffee grinder until a fine powder is achieved, then transfer the powder to a small bowl.
Add 1 cup of hot water (not boiling) to the herbal powder and stir well to create a paste.
Allow the paste to cool, then form small, equal-sized capsules using a capsule maker tool.
Dry the completed capsules in a dehydrator at 105°F (40°C) for 4 hours, then store in an airtight container in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair in the digestive tract. The herb's active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium that is commonly associated with peptic ulcers. By reducing inflammation and eliminating H. pylori, St. John's wort capsule can help alleviate symptoms of peptic ulcers, such as abdominal pain, bloating, and indigestion, and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort has antimicrobial properties that can help kill the Helicobacter pylori bacteria, which is often associated with peptic ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Wound healing: St. John's wort has wound healing properties that can help promote the healing of the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they involve damage to the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and tannins.

Pain relief: St. John's wort has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the pain associated with peptic ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.

Protective: St. John's wort has protective properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage, which is important for peptic ulcers as they involve damage to the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining caused by peptic ulcers.
Leaves: Rich in phenolic compounds and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess wound healing and anti-ulcer effects, making them beneficial for treating peptic ulcers.

Aerial parts: While not a specific part, aerial parts of St. John's Wort (excluding stems) are often used in herbal medicine and have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered St. John's Wort with an equal amount of slippery elm bark powder, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
Add just enough distilled water to the powder mixture to create a thick, dough-like consistency.
Form the dough into small, pea-sized capsules using a capsule maker or by hand, and allow them to dry for 24-48 hours.
Store the dried St. John's Wort capsules in a cool, dark place until ready to use, and take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily as needed for peptic ulcer symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains active compounds, such as flavonoids and essential oils, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby promoting healing of peptic ulcers. Additionally, German chamomile has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with peptic ulcers. Furthermore, it may help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid and other digestive juices, preventing the formation of new ulcers. Overall, German chamomile capsule can be an effective natural remedy for managing peptic ulcers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from pain and irritation caused by peptic ulcers. This is mainly due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of German chamomile help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healing of peptic ulcers. The antioxidants include apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin.

Antimicrobial: German chamomile exhibits antimicrobial properties that help combat Helicobacter pylori, a bacterium commonly associated with peptic ulcers. The main antimicrobial constituents are α-bisabolol and matricin.

Mucosal Protectant: German chamomile acts as a mucosal protectant, forming a protective layer on the stomach lining, preventing acid and digestive enzymes from causing further damage to the ulcerated area. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Anti-secretory: German chamomile has anti-secretory properties that help reduce gastric acid secretion, providing relief from acid reflux and preventing the aggravation of peptic ulcers. This is due to the presence of active constituents like azulene and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the mucous membrane of the stomach, making it beneficial for treating peptic ulcers.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins that have astringent properties, which can help protect the stomach lining and promote healing of peptic ulcers.
Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile contain mucilage, which can form a protective coating on the stomach lining and help reduce irritation caused by peptic ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried German chamomile flowers and size "00" empty capsules, ensuring the flowers are organic and the capsules are vegetarian.
Measure 3 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill each empty capsule with the ground chamomile powder using a capsule-filling machine or by hand, ensuring a consistent dosage of approximately 500mg per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to six months.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water. Consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsule helps with peptic ulcers because it contains rosmarinic acid, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from the pain and discomfort caused by peptic ulcers. Moreover, lemon balm has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help protect the stomach lining from acid and digestive enzymes. Additionally, lemon balm has been found to have antibacterial properties, which may help to eliminate the Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of peptic ulcers. Overall, lemon balm capsules can help to soothe and heal peptic ulcers, promoting digestive health and providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Antimicrobial: Lemon balm has antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate the H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of peptic ulcers. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are the volatile oils, including citral and citronellal.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of peptic ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and rutin.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm has anxiolytic properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate peptic ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and citral.

Mucoprotective: Lemon balm has mucoprotective properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stomach acid, which is important for peptic ulcers as they are often characterized by excessive stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in medicinal tea for peptic ulcers due to their anti-inflammatory and healing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining and promote healing in peptic ulcers.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-ulcer effects and may help reduce inflammation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves, carefully rinsing them to remove any dirt or impurities; allow leaves to dry completely (approximately 12-24 hours).
Grind dried lemon balm leaves into a fine powder using a clean, dedicated coffee or spice grinder (or by hand with a mortar and pestle), ensuring a consistent texture.
Measure out 500mg of the lemon balm powder and combine it with an equal amount of a suitable filler material, such as microcrystalline cellulose or tapioca starch.
Fill size "00" vegetarian cellulose capsules with the lemon balm-filler mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand, ensuring an even distribution of the powder.
Store the completed lemon balm capsules in airtight containers away from direct sunlight, heat, or moisture, and consume 1-2 capsules daily after meals for peptic ulcer relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat peptic ulcers with herbal capsules is by using a combination of slippery elm, marshmallow root, and deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL). These herbs can promote healing of the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and increase mucus production. Additionally, goldenseal and geranium can help to kill harmful bacteria, such as H. pylori, that can cause peptic ulcers. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to peptic ulcers that are treated with herbal capsules are gastritis, acid reflux, and stomach inflammation. These conditions can cause symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and heartburn. Herbal capsules may contain ingredients like slippery elm, licorice root, or marshmallow root, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking herbal capsules to ensure they are safe and effective for your specific condition. Additionally, some herbs can interact with medications, so it is crucial to disclose all supplements and medications you are taking to your healthcare provider.
